entities.name: INDIAN PEANUT CLUMP FUROVIRUS
entities.description: A virus that causes clump disease in peanuts, specifically the IPCV strain.

entities.name: PEANUT CLUMP VIRUS
entities.description: A virus from West Africa related to the Indian peanut clump furovirus.

entities.name: FUROVIRUSES
entities.description: A genus of viruses that includes IPCV and PCV.

entities.name: TOBAMOVIRUSES
entities.description: A group of viruses unrelated to furoviruses used for sequence comparison.

entities.name: TOBRAVIRUSES
entities.description: A group of viruses unrelated to furoviruses used for sequence comparison.

entities.name: BARLEY STRIPE MOSAIC HORDEIVIRUS
entities.description: A hordeivirus that shows unusual coat protein sequence similarity to IPCV and PCV.

entities.name: RNA-2
entities.description: The second RNA segment of the Indian peanut clump furovirus genome.

entities.name: COAT PROTEIN
entities.description: The protein that forms the protective outer shell of a virus.

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The geographic region where the peanut clump virus (PCV) is found.

entities.name: AMINO ACID SEQUENCE
entities.description: The specific order of amino acids that make up a protein.
